arts.ttl: Coventry City's stance on loan market amid concerns of Robins being left short
arts.sum: He added: “But if you have good loan players from top clubs then they can make a difference, as they have done in the past, and obviously we have converted some of them into permanents, namely Callum O’Hare being one of them. That season’s squad also included three of the club’s most successful players in years in Viktor Gyokeres, Callum O’Hare and Ben Sheaf, who also started out as loans before upgrading to permanent contracts.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins clarifies Coventry City criticism & building towards top six
arts.sum: That’s fair enough but what I was talking about was that when we made the changes and the new players come on, and there were three out of the five substitutes, sometimes they are not making the right movements to break down a block that Norwich put in place, but also gave them a leg up in terms of enabling them to keep possession for too long. Tatsu has only missed half a game against Norwich but when they are out, you miss them, and in terms of the training sessions, you want them involved, so it’s a step in the right direction.”

arts.ttl: Coventry City boss explains club's stance on the free agent transfer market in wake of Matty James link
arts.sum: Asked whether the club are looking at any free agents, Robins said: “I don’t think it’s an option. Mark Robins has revealed that dipping into the free agent transfer market is not an option for him at Coventry City at this moment in time.

arts.ttl: Coventry City injury decision pending on man who 'knits everything together'
arts.sum: “He’s on the grass and he’s training so as that’s as close as he has been for a good few weeks,” said Robins, speaking about the influential midfielder who has only played 20 minutes of competitive football so far this term due to pre-season ankle ligament damage. Mark Robins has two of Coventry City’s three injured seniors players back in training but will make a final decision on their availability for Saturday’s trip to Watford after today’s final preparation session at Ryton.

arts.ttl: Former Watford midfielder Capoue makes a totally unexpected move
arts.sum: The French midfielder was subject of a highly speculative rumour to rejoin Watford in the summer, but instead has switched sports totally.
